{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\n\n# Session Workflow\n\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\n\n---\n\n## 1. READ — Load Context\n\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\n\n---\n\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\n\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\n\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\n\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\n\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \"No production environment detected\" in the session log.\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\n\n**If production issues are found:**\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\n\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\n\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\n\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\n\n### 2c. PRD Sync\n\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\n\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\n\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\n\n---\n\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\n\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\n- What you will work on this session and why\n\nSelect work in this priority order:\n\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \"Auto-approving after no objection.\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\n\n---\n\n## 4. ACT — Execute\n\nFor each piece of work:\n1. Implement the change\n2. Run lint, tests, build\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\n\n---\n\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\n\nIf you deployed anything this session:\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\n\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\n\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\n\n---\n\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\n\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\n\n```\nREAD\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\n\nREVIEW\n[ ] Production health checked\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\n\nDECIDE\n[ ] Triage summary written\n[ ] Work selected by priority\n\nACT\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\n\nVERIFY\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\n\nDOCUMENT\n[ ] Session log written\n[ ] Memory updated\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","uuid":"5b3964d1-e67a-4620-b5a6-b2ec752731f7","timestamp":"2026-03-19T22:00:07.765Z","isSynthetic":true}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016t6D4SXzqpWbS2Z8xYGD5g","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","uuid":"832405f9-dcdc-48ec-9d33-84e80071f253","timestamp":"2026-03-19T22:00:13.240Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

Consider deployment setup\n    37→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","uuid":"8aacb518-73d6-4515-af33-7e6b36cc997e","timestamp":"2026-03-19T22:00:13.798Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md","content":"# Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- Core MVP merged to main (PR #15)\n- All 7 P0 issues (#1-#7) completed\n- P1 work in progress: #8, #9 have open PRs\n\n## Open PRs\n- #16: Search and Filters (feat/search-filters) — closes #8\n- #17: Telegram Group Creation Assistance (feat/telegram-assistance) — closes #9\n\n## Open Issues\n- P1: #8 (Search/Filters — PR #16), #9 (Telegram Assistance — PR #17), #10 (Extended Profile)\n- P2: #11-#14 (Telegram auto-create, approval system, ratings, Discord)\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix\n- agent-browser consistently crashes in this container (Chromium memory) — verify with curl instead\n- Playwright chromium installed at user cache dir\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- No production environment deployed yet\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Merge PRs #16 and #17 (1 session elapsed, no objections)\n2. Start on P1 #10 (Extended User Profile)\n3. Consider deployment setup\n","numLines":37,"startLine":1,"totalLines":37}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NQfZeJWMFnRkWyovThAqwA","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser crashes consistently in this container environment (\"Page crashed\")\n    33→- Workaround: use `curl` to verify HTML output and API responses\n    34→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    35→\n    36→## Working Directory\n    37→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    38→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    39→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    40→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","uuid":"7531bd57-7828-4bc3-b0ad-30db0c32e24e","timestamp":"2026-03-19T22:00:14.089Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser crashes consistently in this container environment (\"Page crashed\")\n- Workaround: use `curl` to verify HTML output and API responses\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":40,"startLine":1,"totalLines":40}}}

   48→{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\\n\\n# Session Workflow\\n\\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. READ — Load Context\\n\\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\\n\\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\\n\\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\\n\\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\\n\\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \\\"No production environment detected\\\" in the session log.\\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\\n\\n**If production issues are found:**\\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\\n\\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\\n\\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\\n\\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\\n\\n### 2c. PRD Sync\\n\\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\\n\\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\\n\\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\\n\\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\\n- What you will work on this session and why\\n\\nSelect work in this priority order:\\n\\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \\\"Auto-approving after no objection.\\\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. ACT — Execute\\n\\nFor each piece of work:\\n1. Implement the change\\n2. Run lint, tests, build\\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\\n\\nIf you deployed anything this session:\\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\\n\\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\\n\\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\\n\\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\\n\\n```\\nREAD\\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\\n\\nREVIEW\\n[ ] Production health checked\\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\\n\\nDECIDE\\n[ ] Triage summary written\\n[ ] Work selected by priority\\n\\nACT\\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\\n\\nVERIFY\\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\\n\\nDOCUMENT\\n[ ] Session log written\\n[ ] Memory updated\\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2\",\"uuid\":\"5b3964d1-e67a-4620-b5a6-b2ec752731f7\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-19T22:00:07.765Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n skill: session-workflow\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2\",\"uuid\":\"f4b8d14a-36a1-4833-97c3-bf87e5e2f9e4\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-19T22:00:07.766Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"success\":true,\"commandName\":\"session-workflow\"}}\n{\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Base directory for this skill: /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude/skills/session-workflow\\n\\n# Session Workflow\\n\\nThis skill expands each step of the core loop: **READ → REVIEW → DECIDE → ACT → VERIFY → DOCUMENT**.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. READ — Load Context\\n\\n- Check if `REPOSITORY.md` exists. If not, invoke the `repo-bootstrap` skill before proceeding.\\n- Read `.claude-beat/PRD.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`, `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md`\\n- If PRD.md references additional files, read those too\\n- Read the last 2–3 session logs from `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/`\\n- Fetch open issues and PRs via `gh` CLI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. REVIEW — Assess Current State\\n\\nBefore picking new work, check what needs attention right now.\\n\\n### 2a. Production Health Check (every session)\\n\\nIf the project has a production or staging environment:\\n\\n1. Identify the production URL (from memory, `.env`, deploy config, or README). If no production URL can be found, skip the production health check and note \\\"No production environment detected\\\" in the session log.\\n2. Use `which agent-browser` to locate the browser tool. Always attempt browser verification before falling back to non-visual checks. Open the production URL and verify core functionality works.\\n3. Check for visible errors, broken pages, or degraded behavior\\n4. If the platform provides logs or monitoring (Render, Vercel, etc.) — check recent error logs\\n\\n**If production issues are found:**\\n- **Critical** (app down, core flow broken, data loss risk): fix immediately in this session, create a PR, deploy\\n- **Medium** (non-blocking bugs, UI glitches, non-critical errors): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P1`\\n- **Low** (cosmetic, minor warnings): create a GitHub issue labeled `approved`, `bug` with priority `P2`\\n\\n### 2b. Follow Up on Previous Sessions\\n\\nRead the most recent session logs and check for:\\n\\n- **Deployments**: if something was deployed last session, verify it is live and working (part of 2a)\\n- **Human action requests**: if a previous session created a `needs-human` or `blocked` issue, check whether the human has acted on it\\n  - If resolved — acknowledge, close the issue, continue with any unblocked work\\n  - If stale (no response for 2+ sessions) — add a comment on the issue reminding the owner, and note it in the session log\\n- **Open PRs with review comments**: if a PR has unaddressed reviewer feedback, respond and update before opening new PRs\\n- **WIP branches**: if the previous session left work-in-progress, pick it up and finish it\\n\\n### 2c. PRD Sync\\n\\nScan PRD (and any referenced spec files) for features with no corresponding GitHub issue. For each missing feature:\\n\\n- Label: `approved` + `prd` + priority (`P0`/`P1`/`P2` matching the PRD)\\n- Title prefix: `prd:` (e.g., `prd: add CEFR level selector`)\\n- These are pre-approved — no proposal step, no waiting for human reaction\\n\\nSearch existing issues first to avoid duplicates (`gh issue list --state all`). This step runs every session regardless of other work.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. DECIDE — Pick What to Work On\\n\\nWrite a brief triage summary in your session log before starting work:\\n- Production status (healthy / issues found)\\n- Follow-ups resolved or escalated\\n- What you will work on this session and why\\n\\nSelect work in this priority order:\\n\\n1. **Critical production issues** found during REVIEW\\n2. **Approved `P0` issues** — launch blockers, critical bugs, critical PRD features\\n3. **Open PRs needing attention** — review comments, merge-ready PRs, CI failures\\n4. **In-progress work** from previous sessions (WIP branches, draft PRs)\\n5. **Approved `P1` and `P2` issues** — P1 first, then P2\\n6. **Stale proposals** — if a `proposal` issue has had no human reaction for 2+ sessions, change label to `approved` and comment \\\"Auto-approving after no objection.\\\" Never self-approve issues labeled `needs-human` or `blocked`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. ACT — Execute\\n\\nFor each piece of work:\\n1. Implement the change\\n2. Run lint, tests, build\\n3. Test using `agent-browser` skill if UI-related\\n4. Commit and create/update PR (use `pr-lifecycle` skill)\\n5. If the primary item finishes quickly and there is a small, self-contained second item, you may take it on. Do not start a third item — leave it for the next session.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. VERIFY — Post-Deploy Check\\n\\nIf you deployed anything this session:\\n1. Verify the deployment succeeded\\n2. If browser testing tooling is available, open production and confirm the change is live\\n3. Check for regressions — test core flows, not just the new feature\\n\\nIf you opened or updated a PR this session:\\n- Verify CI checks are passing before closing the session\\n\\nIf you did NOT deploy but production had issues in REVIEW:\\n- Confirm your fix PR is open and ready for review\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. DOCUMENT — Close the Session\\n\\n1. Write session log to `.claude-beat/logs/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM.md`\\n2. Update `.claude-beat/memory/MEMORY.md`\\n3. Update `.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md` if you learned something new\\n4. Ensure no uncommitted changes remain (`git status`)\\n5. All branches pushed, all work has a PR (draft is fine for WIP)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Reference: Session Checklist\\n\\n```\\nREAD\\n[ ] Context loaded (PRD, memory, logs, issues, PRs)\\n\\nREVIEW\\n[ ] Production health checked\\n[ ] Previous session follow-ups addressed\\n\\nDECIDE\\n[ ] Triage summary written\\n[ ] Work selected by priority\\n\\nACT\\n[ ] Changes implemented, tested, PR opened\\n\\nVERIFY\\n[ ] CI checks passing (if PR opened)\\n[ ] Production check (if deployed)\\n\\nDOCUMENT\\n[ ] Session log written\\n[ ] Memory updated\\n[ ] No uncommitted changes\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2\",\"uuid\":\"5b3964d1-e67a-4620-b5a6-b2ec752731f7\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-19T22:00:07.765Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"stream_event\",\"event\":{\"type\":\"message_stop\"},\"session_id\":\"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2\",\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"uuid\":\"0bad5eff-7728-42eb-9293-77a84f157099\"}\n{\"type\":\"rate_limit_event\",\"rate_limit_info\":{\"status\":\"allowed\",\"resetsAt\":1773968400,\"rateLimitType\":\"five_hour\",\"overageStatus\":\"rejected\",\"overageDisabledReason\":\"out_of_credits\",\"isUsingOverage\":false},\"uuid\":\"c7b908cf-39ea-4a6a-91d9-490ddd355362\",\"session_id\":\"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2\"}","numLines":50,"startLine":1,"totalLines":385}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Q8kDTQ8TLHacpmDDvcDTQX","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session: 2026-03-19 21:30 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Triage Summary\n     4→- **Production**: No production environment deployed yet\n     5→- **PR #15 (Core MVP)**: Merged to main — all P0 complete\n     6→- **Open PRs**: None at start of session\n     7→- **PRD Sync**: All features have corresponding issues (#1-#14)\n     8→- **Work selected**: P1 issues — #8 (Search/Filters) and #9 (Telegram Assistance)\n     9→\n    10→## Work Done\n    11→\n    12→### 1. Search and Filters (#8) — PR #16\n    13→- Added search by book title/author with 400ms debounced input\n    14→- Added collapsible filter panel: meeting format dropdown, sort options (newest/oldest/start date)\n    15→- API route accepts `q`, `meeting_format`, `reading_pace`, `sort` query params\n    16→- Dynamic SQL WHERE clause with parameterized queries (safe from injection)\n    17→- Result count display, contextual empty states (filtered vs unfiltered)\n    18→- Active filter indicator dot on Filters button\n    19→- Branch: `feat/search-filters`\n    20→\n    21→### 2. Telegram Group Creation Assistance (#9) — PR #17\n    22→- Added step-by-step instructions (7 steps) for creating a Telegram group\n    23→- Contextual tips: suggested group name using the book title\n    24→- Client-side link validation with real-time visual feedback (green/red)\n    25→- Save button disabled until valid `https://t.me/` link entered\n    26→- Improved waiting state for non-author members with organizer name\n    27→- Added Telegram icon to \"Join Telegram Group\" button\n    28→- Branch: `feat/telegram-assistance`\n    29→\n    30→## Verification\n    31→- Both PRs build successfully (`npm run build`)\n    32→- API tested with curl — filters work correctly\n    33→- agent-browser crashed consistently (Chromium memory issue in container)\n    34→- HTML output verified via curl showing correct render\n    35→\n    36→## Issues\n    37→- agent-browser crashes on `page.goto` with \"Page crashed\" — likely container memory constraints\n    38→- Previous session logs are raw JSON streams (not structured markdown)\n    39→\n    40→## Next Session Priority\n    41→1. Search and Filters (#8) — PR #16\n- Added search by book title/author with 400ms debounced input\n- Added collapsible filter panel: meeting format dropdown, sort options (newest/oldest/start date)\n- API route accepts `q`, `meeting_format`, `reading_pace`, `sort` query params\n- Dynamic SQL WHERE clause with parameterized queries (safe from injection)\n- Result count display, contextual empty states (filtered vs unfiltered)\n- Active filter indicator dot on Filters button\n- Branch: `feat/search-filters`\n\n### 2. Telegram Group Creation Assistance (#9) — PR #17\n- Added step-by-step instructions (7 steps) for creating a Telegram group\n- Contextual tips: suggested group name using the book title\n- Client-side link validation with real-time visual feedback (green/red)\n- Save button disabled until valid `https://t.me/` link entered\n- Improved waiting state for non-author members with organizer name\n- Added Telegram icon to \"Join Telegram Group\" button\n- Branch: `feat/telegram-assistance`\n\n## Verification\n- Both PRs build successfully (`npm run build`)\n- API tested with curl — filters work correctly\n- agent-browser crashed consistently (Chromium memory issue in container)\n- HTML output verified via curl showing correct render\n\n## Issues\n- agent-browser crashes on `page.goto` with \"Page crashed\" — likely container memory constraints\n- Previous session logs are raw JSON streams (not structured markdown)\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Merge PRs #16 and #17 after 1 session (no objections)\n2. Start on #10 (Extended User Profile) — last P1 issue\n3. Consider deployment setup\n","numLines":44,"startLine":1,"totalLines":44}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0114z2AV91bpkcrKbjLEggrc","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState } from \"react\";\n     4→import { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     5→\n     6→export default function ProfilePage() {\n     7→  const router = useRouter();\n     8→  const [displayName, setDisplayName] = useState(\"\");\n     9→  const [bio, setBio] = useState(\"\");\n    10→  const [email, setEmail] = useState(\"\");\n    11→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    12→  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false);\n    13→  const [message, setMessage] = useState(\"\");\n    14→\n    15→  useEffect(() => {\n    16→    fetch(\"/api/auth/me\")\n    17→      .then((r) => r.json())\n    18→      .then((data) => {\n    19→        if (!data.user) {\n    20→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    21→          return;\n    22→        }\n    23→        setDisplayName(data.user.displayName);\n    24→        setBio(data.user.bio || \"\");\n    25→        setEmail(data.user.email);\n    26→        setLoading(false);\n    27→      });\n    28→  }, [router]);\n    29→\n    30→  const handleSave = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    31→    e.preventDefault();\n    32→    setSaving(true);\n    33→    setMessage(\"\");\n    34→\n    35→    try {\n    36→      const res = await fetch(\"/api/profile\", {\n    37→        method: \"PATCH\",\n    38→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    39→        body: JSON.stringify({ displayName, bio }),\n    40→      });\n    41→      const data = await res.json();\n    42→      if (res.ok) {\n    43→        setMessage(\"Profile updated!\");\n    44→      } else {\n    45→        setMessage(data.error || \"Failed to update\");\n    46→      }\n    47→    } catch {\n    48→      setMessage(\"Something went wrong\");\n    49→    } finally {\n    50→      setSaving(false);\n    51→    }\n    52→  };\n    53→\n    54→  if (loading) {\n    55→    return (\n    56→      <div className=\"text-center py-16\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}>\n    57→        <p style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>Loading...</p>\n    58→      </div>\n    59→    );\n    60→  }\n    61→\n    62→  return (\n    63→    <div className=\"max-w-md mx-auto\">\n    64→      <h1 className=\"text-3xl mb-6\">Your Profile</h1>\n    65→\n    66→      <form onSubmit={handleSave} className=\"card space-y-4\">\n    67→        <div>\n    68→          <label\n    69→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n    70→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    71→          >\n    72→            Email\n    73→          </label>\n    74→          <input\n    75→            type=\"email\"\n    76→            className=\"input-field\"\n    77→            value={email}\n    78→            disabled\n    79→            style={{ opacity: 0.6 }}\n    80→          />\n    81→        </div>\n    82→\n    83→        <div>\n    84→          <label\n    85→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n    86→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    87→          >\n    88→            Display name\n    89→          </label>\n    90→          <input\n    91→            type=\"text\"\n    92→            className=\"input-field\"\n    93→            value={displayName}\n    94→            onChange={(e) => setDisplayName(e.target.value)}\n    95→            required\n    96→          />\n    97→        </div>\n    98→\n    99→        <div>\n   100→          <label\n   101→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   102→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   103→          >\n   104→            Bio\n   105→          </label>\n   106→          <textarea\n   107→            className=\"input-field\"\n   108→            rows={3}\n   109→            value={bio}\n   110→            onChange={(e) => setBio(e.target.value)}\n   111→            placeholder=\"Tell other readers about yourself...\"\n   112→          />\n   113→        </div>\n   114→\n   115→        {message && (\n   116→          <p\n   117→            className=\"text-sm\"\n   118→            style={{\n   119→              color: message.includes(\"updated\")\n   120→                ? \"var(--color-success)\"\n   121→                : \"var(--color-error)\",\n   122→              fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   123→            }}\n   124→          >\n   125→            {message}\n   126→          </p>\n   127→        )}\n   128→\n   129→        <button\n   130→          type=\"submit\"\n   131→          className=\"btn-primary w-full\"\n   132→          disabled={saving}\n   133→        >\n   134→          {saving ? \"Saving...\" : \"Save changes\"}\n   135→        </button>\n   136→      </form>\n   137→    </div>\n   138→  );\n   139→}\n   140→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","uuid":"5e3634fe-eba4-4ca9-ae97-c719ab8390d7","timestamp":"2026-03-19T22:01:15.582Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/profile/page.tsx","content":"\"use client\";\n\nimport { useEffect, useState } from \"react\";\nimport { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n\nexport default function ProfilePage() {\n  const router = useRouter();\n  const [displayName, setDisplayName] = useState(\"\");\n  const [bio, setBio] = useState(\"\");\n  const [email, setEmail] = useState(\"\");\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false);\n  const [message, setMessage] = useState(\"\");\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    fetch(\"/api/auth/me\")\n      .then((r) => r.json())\n      .then((data) => {\n        if (!data.user) {\n          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n          return;\n        }\n        setDisplayName(data.user.displayName);\n        setBio(data.user.bio || \"\");\n        setEmail(data.user.email);\n        setLoading(false);\n      });\n  }, [router]);\n\n  const handleSave = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    e.preventDefault();\n    setSaving(true);\n    setMessage(\"\");\n\n    try {\n      const res = await fetch(\"/api/profile\", {\n        method: \"PATCH\",\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({ displayName, bio }),\n      });\n      const data = await res.json();\n      if (res.ok) {\n        setMessage(\"Profile updated!\");\n      } else {\n        setMessage(data.error || \"Failed to update\");\n      }\n    } catch {\n      setMessage(\"Something went wrong\");\n    } finally {\n      setSaving(false);\n    }\n  };\n\n  if (loading) {\n    return (\n      <div className=\"text-center py-16\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}>\n        <p style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>Loading...</p>\n      </div>\n    );\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"max-w-md mx-auto\">\n      <h1 className=\"text-3xl mb-6\">Your Profile</h1>\n\n      <form onSubmit={handleSave} className=\"card space-y-4\">\n        <div>\n          <label\n            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n          >\n            Email\n          </label>\n          <input\n            type=\"email\"\n            className=\"input-field\"\n            value={email}\n            disabled\n            style={{ opacity: 0.6 }}\n          />\n        </div>\n\n        <div>\n          <label\n            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n          >\n            Display name\n          </label>\n          <input\n            type=\"text\"\n            className=\"input-field\"\n            value={displayName}\n            onChange={(e) => setDisplayName(e.target.value)}\n            required\n          />\n        </div>\n\n        <div>\n          <label\n            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n          >\n            Bio\n          </label>\n          <textarea\n            className=\"input-field\"\n            rows={3}\n            value={bio}\n            onChange={(e) => setBio(e.target.value)}\n            placeholder=\"Tell other readers about yourself...\"\n          />\n        </div>\n\n        {message && (\n          <p\n            className=\"text-sm\"\n            style={{\n              color: message.includes(\"updated\")\n                ? \"var(--color-success)\"\n                : \"var(--color-error)\",\n              fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n            }}\n          >\n            {message}\n          </p>\n        )}\n\n        <button\n          type=\"submit\"\n          className=\"btn-primary w-full\"\n          disabled={saving}\n        >\n          {saving ? \"Saving...\" : \"Save changes\"}\n        </button>\n      </form>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n","numLines":140,"startLine":1,"totalLines":140}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HtFYkyryFX8FbYrnY57b2U","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n     6→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     7→  if (!session) {\n     8→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n     9→  }\n    10→\n    11→  const { displayName, bio } = await req.json();\n    12→\n    13→  if (!displayName || displayName.trim().length === 0) {\n    14→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Display name is required\" }, { status: 400 });\n    15→  }\n    16→\n    17→  db.prepare(\"UPDATE users SET display_name = ?, bio = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n    18→    displayName.trim(),\n    19→    (bio || \"\").trim(),\n    20→    session.userId\n    21→  );\n    22→\n    23→  // Update session\n    24→  session.displayName = displayName.trim();\n    25→  await session.save();\n    26→\n    27→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    28→}\n    29→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","uuid":"afa92795-71d3-4adb-b27d-329b8f82d06e","timestamp":"2026-03-19T22:01:15.701Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\nexport async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { displayName, bio } = await req.json();\n\n  if (!displayName || displayName.trim().length === 0) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Display name is required\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  db.prepare(\"UPDATE users SET display_name = ?, bio = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n    displayName.trim(),\n    (bio || \"\").trim(),\n    session.userId\n  );\n\n  // Update session\n  session.displayName = displayName.trim();\n  await session.save();\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n","numLines":29,"startLine":1,"totalLines":29}}}

20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    58→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    59→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    60→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    61→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    62→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    63→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    64→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    65→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    66→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport path from \"path\";\nimport fs from \"fs\";\n\nconst DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\nconst DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n\n// Ensure data directory exists\nif (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n}\n\nconst db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n\n// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\ndb.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\ndb.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n\n// Initialize schema\ndb.exec(`\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    listing_id INTEGER,\n    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n  );\n`);\n\nexport default db;\n","numLines":71,"startLine":1,"totalLines":71}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0122KSn6RhPug5oFTxbmdtLm","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→\n     6→interface Listing {\n     7→  id: number;\n     8→  book_title: string;\n     9→  book_author: string;\n    10→  book_cover_url: string;\n    11→  language: string;\n    12→  reading_pace: string;\n    13→  start_date: string;\n    14→  meeting_format: string;\n    15→  max_group_size: number;\n    16→  member_count: number;\n    17→  author_name: string;\n    18→  created_at: string;\n    19→}\n    20→\n    21→export default function HomePage() {\n    22→  const [listings, setListings] = useState<Listing[]>([]);\n    23→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    24→\n    25→  // Search & filter state\n    26→  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    27→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"\");\n    28→  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"newest\");\n    29→  const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = useState(false);\n    30→\n    31→  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    32→\n    33→  const fetchListings = useCallback(\n    34→    (query: string, format: string, sortBy: string) => {\n    35→      setLoading(true);\n    36→      const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    37→      if (query) params.set(\"q\", query);\n    38→      if (format) params.set(\"meeting_format\", format);\n    39→      if (sortBy) params.set(\"sort\", sortBy);\n    40→\n    41→      const qs = params.toString();\n    42→      fetch(`/api/listings${qs ? `?${qs}` : \"\"}`)\n    43→        .then((r) => r.json())\n    44→        .then((data) => {\n    45→          setListings(data.listings || []);\n    46→          setLoading(false);\n    47→        })\n    48→        .catch(() => setLoading(false));\n    49→    },\n    50→    []"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","uuid":"ab7f7a9b-250e-46c3-9d63-9889719ae169","timestamp":"2026-03-19T22:01:24.685Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx","content":"\"use client\";\n\nimport { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\nimport Link from \"next/link\";\n\ninterface Listing {\n  id: number;\n  book_title: string;\n  book_author: string;\n  book_cover_url: string;\n  language: string;\n  reading_pace: string;\n  start_date: string;\n  meeting_format: string;\n  max_group_size: number;\n  member_count: number;\n  author_name: string;\n  created_at: string;\n}\n\nexport default function HomePage() {\n  const [listings, setListings] = useState<Listing[]>([]);\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n\n  // Search & filter state\n  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(\"\");\n  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"\");\n  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"newest\");\n  const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = useState(false);\n\n  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n\n  const fetchListings = useCallback(\n    (query: string, format: string, sortBy: string) => {\n      setLoading(true);\n      const params = new URLSearchParams();\n      if (query) params.set(\"q\", query);\n      if (format) params.set(\"meeting_format\", format);\n      if (sortBy) params.set(\"sort\", sortBy);\n\n      const qs = params.toString();\n      fetch(`/api/listings${qs ? `?${qs}` : \"\"}`)\n        .then((r) => r.json())\n        .then((data) => {\n          setListings(data.listings || []);\n          setLoading(false);\n        })\n        .catch(() => setLoading(false));\n    },\n    []","numLines":50,"startLine":1,"totalLines":434}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PwBFmFoh8R8Jbd41FqpVz5","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→@import \"tailwindcss\";\n     2→\n     3→:root {\n     4→ \"tailwindcss\";\n\n:root {\n  --color-bg: #faf7f2;\n  --color-surface: #ffffff;\n  --color-text: #1a1a1a;\n  --color-text-secondary: #6b6b6b;\n  --color-accent: #e07a3a;\n  --color-accent-hover: #c96a2e;\n  --color-border: #e5e0da;\n  --color-success: #2d8a56;\n  --color-error: #c0392b;\n}\n\nbody {\n  background-color: var(--color-bg);\n  color: var(--color-text);\n  font-family: 'Georgia', 'Times New Roman', serif;\n}\n\nh1, h2, h3, h4 {\n  font-family: 'Georgia', serif;\n  font-weight: 700;\n  letter-spacing: -0.02em;\n}\n\ninput, select, textarea {\n  font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;\n}\n\n.btn-primary {\n  background-color: var(--color-accent);\n  color: white;\n  padding: 0.625rem 1.5rem;\n  border-radius: 0.5rem;\n  font-weight: 600;\n  font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;\n  font-size: 0.9rem;\n  transition: background-color 0.15s;\n  cursor: pointer;\n  border: none;\n}\n\n.btn-primary:hover {\n  background-color: var(--color-accent-hover);\n}\n\n.btn-primary:disabled {\n  opacity: 0.5;\n  cursor: not-allowed;\n}\n\n.btn-secondary {\n  background-color: transparent;\n  color: var(--color-text);\n  padding: 0.625rem 1.5rem;\n  border-radius: 0.5rem;\n  font-weight: 600;\n  font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;\n  font-size: 0.9rem;\n  border: 1.5px solid var(--color-border);\n  transition: border-color 0.15s, background-color 0.15s;\n  cursor: pointer;\n}\n\n.btn-secondary:hover {\n  border-color: var(--color-text-secondary);\n  background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.02);\n}\n\n.card {\n  background-color: var(--color-surface);\n  border: 1px solid var(--color-border);\n  border-radius: 0.75rem;\n  padding: 1.5rem;\n}\n\n.input-field {\n  width: 100%;\n  padding: 0.625rem 0.875rem;\n  border: 1.5px solid var(--color-border);\n  border-radius: 0.5rem;\n  font-size: 0.95rem;\n  background: var(--color-surface);\n  transition: border-color 0.15s;\n}\n\n.input-field:focus {\n  outline: none;\n  border-color: var(--color-accent);\n  box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(224, 122, 58, 0.1);\n}\n\n.badge {\n  display: inline-flex;\n  align-items: center;\n  padding: 0.25rem 0.625rem;\n  border-radius: 9999px;\n  font-size: 0.75rem;\n  font-weight: 600;\n  font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;\n}\n","numLines":103,"startLine":1,"totalLines":103}}}

\"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\",\n    14→  cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    15→  cookieOptions: {\n    16→    httpOnly: true,\n    17→    secure: { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\nimport { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n\nexport interface SessionData {\n  userId?: number;\n  email?: string;\n  displayName?: string;\n}\n\nconst sessionOptions: SessionOptions = {\n  password:\n    process.env.SESSION_SECRET ||\n    \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\",\n  cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n  cookieOptions: {\n    httpOnly: true,\n    secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\",\n    sameSite: \"lax\",\n    maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n  },\n};\n\nexport async function getSession() {\n  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, sessionOptions);\n}\n\nexport async function requireAuth() {\n  const session = await getSession();\n  if (!session.userId) {\n    return null;\n  }\n  return session;\n}\n","numLines":35,"startLine":1,"totalLines":35}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01C4oPG12sdzxsW36oU9QLs3","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","uuid":"0fa6dc58-4de0-48b1-aba3-a00146cddebe","timestamp":"2026-03-19T22:01:37.936Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts","oldString":"  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (","newString":"  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (","originalFile":"import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport path from \"path\";\nimport fs from \"fs\";\n\nconst DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\nconst DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n\n// Ensure data directory exists\nif (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n}\n\nconst db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n\n// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\ndb.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\ndb.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n\n// Initialize schema\ndb.exec(`\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n  );\n\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    listing_id INTEGER,\n    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n  );\n`);\n\nexport default db;\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":54,"oldLines":6,"newStart":54,"newLines":13,"lines":["     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)","   );"," ","+  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (","+    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,","+    genre TEXT NOT NULL,","+    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),","+    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)","+  );","+","   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (","     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,","     user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":0,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"\"}"}},"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"e3f6bd8f-2797-4eac-89c4-09b22fb247d0"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01DZxmoju9D6PWmwqYmLJSBj","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_011JEj7AMrtt1mYRhycCvR7a","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts","content":"import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\ninterface ReadingRow {\n  id: number;\n  book_title: string;\n  book_author: string;\n  book_cover_url: string;\n  reading_pace: string;\n  start_date: string;\n  meeting_format: string;\n  is_full: number;\n  telegram_link: string;\n  member_count: number;\n  max_group_size: number;\n  author_name: string;\n  joined_at: string;\n}\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  // Get all listings the user is a member of (including ones they authored)\n  const readings = db.prepare(`\n    SELECT\n      l.id,\n      l.book_title,\n      l.book_author,\n      l.book_cover_url,\n      l.reading_pace,\n      l.start_date,\n      l.meeting_format,\n      l.is_full,\n      l.telegram_link,\n      l.max_group_size,\n      u.display_name as author_name,\n      lm.joined_at,\n      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    FROM listing_members lm\n    JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n    JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id\n    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n\n  // Currently reading: groups that are full (active) or start_date <= today\n  const currentlyReading = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today\n  );\n\n  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n  const history = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 1 && r.start_date < today\n  );\n\n  const upcoming = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 0 && r.start_date > today\n  );\n\n  const active = readings.filter(\n    (r) => !history.includes(r) && !upcoming.includes(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n  );\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    active,\n    history,\n    upcoming,\n    total: readings.length,\n  });\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":238,"cache_read_input_tokens":38428,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":238},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","uuid":"1e9987ea-2f88-4124-9b82-7c476bb73b91"}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":1,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\"file_pat"}},"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"da123e54-b6dd-4b40-ab5d-33b793ef8e7e"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011JEj7AMrtt1mYRhycCvR7a","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","uuid":"ecec3944-bcff-4b04-9aca-f11b3b048abb","timestamp":"2026-03-19T22:01:57.449Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"create","filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts","content":"import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\ninterface ReadingRow {\n  id: number;\n  book_title: string;\n  book_author: string;\n  book_cover_url: string;\n  reading_pace: string;\n  start_date: string;\n  meeting_format: string;\n  is_full: number;\n  telegram_link: string;\n  member_count: number;\n  max_group_size: number;\n  author_name: string;\n  joined_at: string;\n}\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  // Get all listings the user is a member of (including ones they authored)\n  const readings = db.prepare(`\n    SELECT\n      l.id,\n      l.book_title,\n      l.book_author,\n      l.book_cover_url,\n      l.reading_pace,\n      l.start_date,\n      l.meeting_format,\n      l.is_full,\n      l.telegram_link,\n      l.max_group_size,\n      u.display_name as author_name,\n      lm.joined_at,\n      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    FROM listing_members lm\n    JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n    JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id\n    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n\n  // Currently reading: groups that are full (active) or start_date <= today\n  const currentlyReading = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today\n  );\n\n  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n  const history = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 1 && r.start_date < today\n  );\n\n  const upcoming = readings.filter(\n    (r) => r.is_full === 0 && r.start_date > today\n  );\n\n  const active = readings.filter(\n    (r) => !history.includes(r) && !upcoming.includes(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n  );\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    active,\n    history,\n    upcoming,\n    total: readings.length,\n  });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01DZxmoju9D6PWmwqYmLJSBj","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01CetQ2hspf4ZEnaPmbZUf3w","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/genres/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\nconst AVAILABLE_GENRES = [\n  \"Literary Fiction\",\n  \"Science Fiction\",\n  \"Fantasy\",\n  \"Mystery\",\n  \"Thriller\",\n  \"Romance\",\n  \"Historical Fiction\",\n  \"Horror\",\n  \"Dystopian\",\n  \"Adventure\",\n  \"Contemporary Fiction\",\n  \"Magical Realism\",\n  \"Classics\",\n  \"Short Stories\",\n  \"Young Adult\",\n  \"Graphic Novels\",\n];\n\ninterface GenreRow {\n  genre: string;\n}\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const userGenres = db.prepare(\n    \"SELECT genre FROM user_genres WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY genre\"\n  ).all(session.userId) as GenreRow[];\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    genres: userGenres.map((g) => g.genre),\n    available: AVAILABLE_GENRES,\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function PUT(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { genres } = await req.json();\n\n  if (!Array.isArray(genres)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"genres must be an array\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Validate all genres\n  const validGenres = genres.filter((g: string) => AVAILABLE_GENRES.includes(g));\n\n  // Replace all genres in a transaction\n  const updateGenres = db.transaction(() => {\n    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM user_genres WHERE user_id = ?\").run(session.userId);\n    const insert = db.prepare(\"INSERT INTO user_genres (user_id, genre) VALUES (?, ?)\");\n    for (const genre of validGenres) {\n      insert.run(session.userId, genre);\n    }\n  });\n\n  updateGenres();\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ genres: validGenres });\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":238,"cache_read_input_tokens":38428,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":238},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2f6cc8c8-268f-4ae8-9362-55232849d2d2","uuid":"409d3cee-f589-4489-976c-0ff86f4f0bd3"}

ORDER BY genre\"\n  ).all(session.userId) as GenreRow[];\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    genres: userGenres.map((g) => g.genre),\n    available: AVAILABLE_GENRES,\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function PUT(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { genres } = await req.json();\n\n  if (!Array.isArray(genres)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"genres must be an array\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Validate all genres\n  const validGenres = genres.filter((g: string) => AVAILABLE_GENRES.includes(g));\n\n  // Replace all genres in a transaction\n  const updateGenres = db.transaction(() => {\n    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM user_genres WHERE user_id = ?\").run(session.userId);\n    const insert = db.prepare(\"INSERT INTO user_genres (user_id, genre) VALUES (?, ?)\");\n    for (const genre of validGenres) {\n      insert.run(session.userId, genre);\n    }\n  });\n\n  updateGenres();\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ genres: validGenres });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
